title: Other Accounts of al-Muhtadi 's Death
description: Someone else said that the beginning of the whole dispute occurred when the sons of the lurks met together and stated that they were not content to allow anyone to be their chief except the Commander of the Faithful. They wrote to Masi b. Bugha and Bayakbak to that effect, while the two were still engaged with the Khirijite (Musiwir). On Friday Masi arrived with his men at a bridge in the district of al-Waziriyyah. Al-Muhtadi was camped with his army in alHayr; he approached them but then turned away and proceeded armed to the Jawsaq palace.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 36
